label: DFG Sachbeihilfe (Individual Research Grant / Research Grants Programme)

sections.title: Part A – Proposal Data and Obligations
sections.purpose: Project metadata, participating persons, and legal obligations entered in the elan portal (not prose).
mustContain: Project metadata and participating persons entered via elan (elan.dfg.de)
Summary of the project in BOTH German and English (German summary mandatory regardless of proposal language)
Legal obligations and declarations

sections.title: Starting point – State of the art and preliminary work
sections.purpose: Position the project against the field, define the gap, and present preliminary work.
mustContain: State of the art described briefly and precisely in DIRECT relationship to the project
Self-contained: understandable WITHOUT reading any cited literature (reviewers are not required to read citations)
Clear statement of the unique, innovative, promising contribution and the gap being closed
Status of own preliminary work, listed with date of publication; reference DFG funding period if DFG-funded
Clear attribution of whose work is referred to (failure can breach good research practice)

sections.title: Objectives and work programme
sections.purpose: State the hypothesis-driven objectives and the detailed work programme, data handling, and diversity relevance.
mustContain: 2.1 Anticipated total duration (max initial funding usually 3 years, then renewal)
2.2 Objectives – concise, hypothesis-driven framing; clear hypotheses/objectives logically derived from the state of knowledge

2.3 Work programme incl. methods, per applicant; schedule of all planned experiments; tasks must correspond to funds requested; methods classified (available / to develop / external); alternative strategies and risk mitigation

2.4 Handling of research data – mandatory DMP per DFG checklist (characteristics/scope, documentation/quality, storage/archiving, legal, reuse/long-term access, responsibilities); if no relevant data, say so explicitly
2.5 Relevance of sex, gender and/or diversity – whether/how relevant to methods/work programme/objectives, or why genuinely not relevant

sections.title: Project- and subject-related list of publications
sections.purpose: List only the works actually cited in sections 1–2.
mustContain: Only works actually cited in sections 1–2 (no total limit)
Font ≥ Arial 9 pt (smaller font allowed only here)
Highlight (bold) a MAXIMUM of 10 of your own most project-relevant publications (cap stays 10 total even with multiple applicants)
Follow form 1.91 for publication lists

sections.title: Supplementary information on the research context
sections.purpose: Ethical/legal aspects, employment, group composition, cooperations, equipment and other declarations (the ≤ 8-page block).
mustContain: 4.1 Ethical and/or legal aspects – risks/harm and mitigation; humans/human material/identifiable data (criteria, sample-size justification, risks, informed consent, ethics vote may be attached); animals (justify model, numbers + statistical design, 3Rs); Nagoya Protocol/ABS for foreign genetic resources; DURC + export control (KEF statement if required)
4.2 Employment status per applicant
4.3 First-time proposal data (optional self-declaration; career stage weighted favourably)
4.4 Composition of the project group (people not paid from project funds)
4.5 Cooperation in Germany
4.6 Cooperation abroad (signed letter of commitment attached)
4.7 Project-relevant collaborations over the past 3 years (conflict-of-interest)
4.8 Commercial enterprises (cooperation)
4.9 Participation in commercial enterprises
4.10 Scientific equipment
4.11 Other submissions
4.12 Other information
If a topic is central to the research question, discuss it in sections 1–2 and merely cross-reference here

sections.title: Requested modules/funds
sections.purpose: Justify each requested item per applicant under the modular funding scheme.
mustContain: Explain each item per applicant; funds must derive cogently from the work programme

Modular funding: Basic Module (direct project costs, project-specific staff, instrumentation) plus Temporary Positions for PIs, Replacement funding, Temporary Clinician Substitutes, Project-Specific Workshops, Mercator Fellows, Public Relations
Follow module guidelines (e.g. form 52.01); staff levels justified by task and proportional to knowledge gain

sections.title: Part C – Attachments (CV and situational documents)
sections.purpose: Mandatory CV per applicant plus situational attachments.
mustContain: One academic CV per applicant using form 53.200 (compulsory template)

CV publication list: Category A (mandatory, peer-reviewed articles/conference/anthology contributions/books) max 10; Category B (optional, preprints/datasets/software/patents) max 10 – need not relate to the project
Career interruptions may be disclosed so reviewers weight the record fairly
No bibliometrics: impact factors and h-indices not required and not to be considered (qualitative assessment)
Situational: ethics votes, host-institution statements, foreign-partner letters of commitment, acceptance letters for in-press papers

reviewCriteria: Scientific excellence, relevance and originality (weighed, considering individual career stage; no non-scientific criteria)

checklist.check: Project Description is ≤ 25 pages total.
Sections 1–3 occupy ≤ 17 pages (no borrowing from the section 4+ budget).
Sections 4 onward occupy ≤ 8 pages (separate budget from sections 1–3).
Body text is ≥ Arial 11 pt (publication list ≥ Arial 9 pt) and line spacing is ≥ 1.2.
All required sections are present and numbered exactly (1, 2.1–2.5, 3, 4.1–4.12, 5).
Section 1 is self-contained – the argument is understandable without reading any cited work.
Section 2.2 states clear, falsifiable hypotheses/objectives derived from section 1.
Objectives, work programme, and budget form a coherent chain: every task maps to a requested funding item.
Section 2.3 has a concrete schedule of planned experiments, classified methods, and alternative strategies / risk mitigation.
Section 2.4 contains a DMP per the DFG checklist, or explicitly states no relevant data is generated.
Section 2.5 genuinely addresses sex/gender/diversity relevance (or states why not relevant).
Section 3 lists only works cited in sections 1–2 and every citation resolves to a real reference.
At most 10 of the applicants' own publications are highlighted in section 3 (10 total even with multiple applicants).
One CV per applicant using form 53.200, with Category A ≤ 10 and Category B ≤ 10 publications.
No impact factors or h-indices are used anywhere (assessment is qualitative).
If generative AI was used for text/images, its use is disclosed appropriately.
Part A includes a project summary in both German and English (German summary mandatory).
Section 4.1 covers applicable ethical/legal aspects (ethics vote, animal 3Rs, Nagoya/ABS, DURC/export) with attachments where required.
Section 5 funds are modular, justified per item, and proportional to knowledge gain.
Each attachment PDF is < 10 MB, unlocked (readable/copyable/printable), and named per DFG protocol; the current elan template is used with formatting/numbering retained.

redFlags: Not self-contained – the argument only works if the reviewer reads the cited papers
Missing or wrong key references (own work or competitors')
Objectives ≠ work programme ≠ budget – the most common substantive weakness
No risk management / no plan B / no alternative methods
Budget over-ask, or staff/co-applicants without a defined role
Over-ambition vs feasibility with thin preliminary work
Bibliometric padding (h-index, impact factors) – explicitly disregarded, signals rule-blindness
Self-plagiarism / unattributed reuse of others' work
Formatting violations (under-size font, over length, locked PDFs)
Empty mandatory sections (data handling; sex/gender/diversity; ethics)
requiredAttachments: One academic CV per applicant (form 53.200, compulsory template) with Category A ≤ 10 and Category B ≤ 10 publications
Ethics-committee vote (if humans/human material/identifiable data are involved)
Signed letters of commitment from cooperation partners abroad (section 4.6)
Acceptance letters for in-press publications cited as preliminary work
KEF statement for DURC/export-control-relevant projects (if required)
recencyNote: Built on form 54.01 v.06/26 and form 10.20 v.04/26. DFG revises forms roughly twice a year – always re-download the elan template per submission and verify the date code (e.g. '54.01 – 06/26'). Re-verify per cycle: page-split rules, the modular funding catalogue (form 52.01), the CV template (form 53.200), the '10 highlighted in proposal list' vs '10+10 CV' distinction, the AI-disclosure policy, and the mandatory data-management and sex/gender/diversity sections. Where German and English versions diverge, the German text legally takes precedence. Submission is via elan only, with no deadlines; only ~1 in 3 proposals can be funded.
